flowRoot.flowPara: Values replicate a test piece that was bent along the channels. It was 90.5 mm flat. Inside measuring tolerance.

text.tspan: Values replicate a test piece that was pent diagonally to the channels. It was 79,92 flat.
Verified with a test diagonally to channels

flowRoot.flowPara: The most common bends tested digonally across the channels.
With a compromise K factor of 0.8 and outside bend radius of R4mm (CAD minimum)

Test for 130 deg bend indicate that it adds 1 mm to the flat pattern dimension vs reality due to the actual outside radius is larger than R4 but it can be easily deformed to "fit". Larger radius = more relaxed.

Test for 90 deg bend indicate that it subtracts 1,5mm from the flat pattern vs reality due to sharper creases and less stretch due to more internal crushing (the K factor increases as the centerline moves out)
